task: Ralph (2026-05-16): Make doable's installer hassle-free for admins. (1) Replace setup wizard Step 2's 3+1 hardcoded provider tiles with a searchable catalog grid backed by the existing 60+ provider PROVIDER_CATALOG; MiniMax must appear as a popular tile. (2) Bake MINIMAX_API_KEY env var into docker-compose.yml + docker/setup.sh + setup-server.sh so an admin who exports the key before install gets MiniMax pre-configured. (3) Clean stale '5-step / build your first app' copy in docker/setup.sh that doesn't match the actual 4-step wizard. (4) Hetzner Robot API discovery script (NON-destructive) that resolves server #2987905 to its IP using ws+ credentials. (5) End-to-end test recipe at C:/Users/gj/Documents/doable-hetzner-recipe.md. (6) Local docker compose smoke run + bug iteration loop. Commits go on main; deploy only to Hetzner #2987905, never do.fid.pw / dodev.fid.pw. Never bind 0.0.0.0. Never weaken security.

stories.title: Searchable provider catalog grid in Step 2 (replaces 3+1 hardcoded tiles)
acceptanceCriteria: apps/web/src/app/setup/steps/Step2AIProvider.tsx imports PROVIDER_CATALOG / PROVIDER_BY_ID from @doable/shared (packages/shared/src/ai/provider-catalog.ts) and renders tiles sourced from that catalog, not a 4-entry hardcoded PROVIDERS array

The default view shows at least 12 popular provider tiles including: anthropic, openai, minimax, groq, openrouter, mistral, deepseek, together, fireworks, ollama, github_copilot, and a 'BYOK custom URL' fallback
A search input filters tiles by provider name or id with case-insensitive substring match
A 'Show all 60+ providers' toggle expands the grid to render the full PROVIDER_CATALOG length (PROVIDER_COUNT)
Selecting a tile pre-fills the form fields from the provider preset: baseUrl, defaultModel, available models list, apiKeyPlaceholder, apiKeyHelpUrl; only the API key input is required from the user

Selecting MiniMax pre-fills baseUrl=https://api.minimax.io/v1, defaultModel=MiniMax-M2.7, and 'Validate & Save' POSTs provider=custom, baseUrl=that URL, model=MiniMax-M2.7 to /api/setup/ai-provider (matching existing aiProviderSchema)
TypeScript compiles cleanly: pnpm -r --filter='./apps/web' exec tsc --noEmit  → 0 errors on Step2AIProvider.tsx
Existing 'Skip for now' link and Back/Continue buttons retained; no visual regression to header/footer of Step 2

stories.title: docker/setup.sh post-install copy reflects the real 4-step wizard
acceptanceCriteria: docker/setup.sh contains zero occurrences of '5-step', 'first app', or 'template gallery'
The post-install banner lists exactly the four real steps from WizardShell.tsx: Welcome, AI Provider, Sign-in, Plans & Billing
Bash syntax check passes: bash -n docker/setup.sh → exit 0

stories.title: MINIMAX_API_KEY env-var seeding across docker stack + setup-server.sh
acceptanceCriteria: docker-compose.yml api service env block has MINIMAX_API_KEY: ${MINIMAX_API_KEY:-} passthrough
docker/setup.sh writes MINIMAX_API_KEY= (empty default) into the AI block of docker/.env so admins can paste their key without editing compose
setup-server.sh (bare-metal path) writes MINIMAX_API_KEY=${MINIMAX_API_KEY:-} into the generated services/api/.env and apps/web/.env when present in the calling environment

When MINIMAX_API_KEY is set at first API boot AND platform_config.setup.ai_provider_key is unset, services/api boot path seeds setup.ai_provider=custom, setup.ai_provider_base_url=https://api.minimax.io/v1, setup.ai_model=MiniMax-M2.7, setup.ai_provider_key=<encrypted via setEncryptedConfig> using a system actor

GET /api/setup/status (as platform admin) after first boot with MINIMAX_API_KEY set reports fields_configured.ai_provider_key=true, ai_provider='custom', ai_provider_base_url='https://api.minimax.io/v1'
No plaintext key ever logged: grep -ri 'sk-cp-' services/api/src 2>/dev/null returns 0 hits

stories.title: Hetzner Robot API discovery script (NON-destructive) for server #2987905
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/hetzner-provision.ps1 exists and accepts -ServerNumber 2987905 -Action discover. Reads HETZNER_ROBOT_USER and HETZNER_ROBOT_PASS from env (never logs them)
Discover mode: GETs https://robot-ws.your-server.de/server, parses JSON, prints { server_number, server_ip, status, dc } for the matching server
Rescue mode (-Action rescue) requires -Confirm switch; otherwise prints what it WOULD do (POST /boot/<ip>/rescue os=linux arch=64) without sending the request
Install mode (-Action installimage) and reset mode (-Action reset) both require -Confirm and additionally refuse to run if ServerNumber is not 2987905 (hard whitelist guard)
Script exits with non-zero status on missing creds, HTTP error, or server not found, and prints a single-line diagnostic to stderr in those cases

stories.title: End-to-end Hetzner test recipe markdown at C:/Users/gj/Documents/doable-hetzner-recipe.md
acceptanceCriteria: C:/Users/gj/Documents/doable-hetzner-recipe.md exists

Contains sections: Prereqs / Discover server IP / Rescue mode + SSH-key paste / installimage Ubuntu 24.04 autosetup / Reboot + reconnect / Clone repo / Run docker/setup.sh with MINIMAX_API_KEY exported / Smoke curl tests / Run testcases subset / Tear down (next reinstall)
Every command block is copy-pasteable verbatim (PowerShell + bash variants where relevant), with placeholder $env:HETZNER_ROBOT_USER / $env:HETZNER_ROBOT_PASS / $env:MINIMAX_API_KEY
Recipe NEVER embeds the real credentials or key — only placeholders + a 'rotate before sharing' warning at the top

stories.title: Local docker compose smoke + auth/api testcase pass
acceptanceCriteria: docker compose --env-file docker/.env up -d --build succeeds on Windows host (or WSL2) — all four containers (postgres, migrate-completed, api, ws, web) report healthy or running
curl http://127.0.0.1:4000/health returns HTTP 200 with JSON body containing 'ok'
First signup at http://127.0.0.1:3000/auth/register succeeds (empty users table → first signup becomes platform owner)
With MINIMAX_API_KEY exported before compose up, /api/setup/status after admin login reports fields_configured.ai_provider_key=true and ai_provider='custom'
Smoke subset of testcases/01-auth passes (>=8 of 10 acceptance cases) and is logged to testcases/99-runlog/DEPLOYLOG.md with UTC timestamp

Any failed test gets a bug entry filed at testcases/bugs/2026-05-16-<area>-<id>.md with steps to reproduce + evidence file path; a dedicated Opus 4.7 executor agent is launched per bug, fixes it, commits, and the smoke subset is rerun until green
